theme_refs.synthesis_note: Predicting pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions appears in 5 evidence unit(s), with 0 gap signal(s) and 0 contradiction signal(s).

supporting_points: Instrumental attitude was a significant predictor of pedestrians' violating crossing behavior intention across all four models tested

When descriptive norm was added to the model, subjective norm became insignificant while descriptive norm emerged as a stronger predictor, indicating that pedestrians' observations of family and friends' behavior had greater influence than perceived approval from them

theme_refs.synthesis_note: Predicting pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions appears in 5 evidence unit(s), with 0 gap signal(s) and 0 contradiction signal(s).

supporting_points: This study investigates how mobile phone distractions (music distraction, phone conversation distraction, and text distraction) affect the behavior of pedestrians while crossing the street at a signalized intersection, addressing the growing safety concern of mobile phone use among pedestrians.

argument_moves.purpose: Anchor the section in 6 extracted claim signal(s) and the strongest matrix evidence.

theme_refs.label: Survey Data From Dalian, China (260 Complete And Valid Responses)
theme_refs.synthesis_note: Survey data from Dalian, China (260 complete and valid responses) appears in 5 evidence unit(s), with 0 gap signal(s) and 0 contradiction signal(s).

theme_refs.label: Predicting Pedestrians' Violating Road Crossing Behavior Intentions
theme_refs.synthesis_note: Predicting pedestrians' violating road-crossing behavior intentions appears in 5 evidence unit(s), with 0 gap signal(s) and 0 contradiction signal(s).

supporting_points: Text distraction causes the greatest impairment to pedestrians' crossing performance, followed by phone conversation distraction and music distraction, with text distraction significantly affecting all three crossing behavior variables and five visual attention variables

Music distraction increases pedestrian pupil diameter and reduces scanning frequency as well as fixation points and fixation times toward traffic signal area priority, indicating cognitive load without extreme behavioral impairment

argument_moves.purpose: Close the section by stating the field-level takeaway rather than repeating paper-by-paper details.
